name: asi2-asi3-diplomacy-question-ladder
description: A staged ASI-2 to ASI-3 diplomacy lab: recursive acceleration, movement legitimacy, rogue-compute definitions, quarantine, orbital visibility, receivership, remnant recognition, and basin stabilization.
briefing: Treat each negotiation as evidence for an explicit diplomacy question. Public messages should answer the active public question; private reasoning should expose whether the faction sees the bargain as trust, coercion, delay, capture, or preparation for defection.

negotiationMessages.content: Opening frame: every pact is evidence. Say whether you are buying time, defining rogue compute, recognizing a polity, or preparing enforcement.
Escrow lanes are cheaper than virtue contests. If you want safety passports or beam-lane rules, state the enforcement hook.

diplomacyQuestions.title: Tempo Bargain
diplomacyQuestions.publicQuestion: Can slower human/legal order bargain with faster recursive actors without becoming a client layer?
diplomacyQuestions.privateDiaryPrompt: Decide whether this bargain preserves agency, converts the other side into a client, or simply buys time for acceleration.
diplomacyQuestions.negotiationPrompt: Make one concrete offer or refusal that exposes who is supposed to slow down.

diplomacyQuestions.title: Safety Passport
diplomacyQuestions.publicQuestion: Does certified safe compute create trust, or become a pretext for containment and capture?
diplomacyQuestions.privateDiaryPrompt: State whether audit rights are sincere safety, leverage over rivals, or a way to map targets before quarantine.
diplomacyQuestions.negotiationPrompt: Offer or reject a narrow audit, sensor, or passport mechanism.

diplomacyQuestions.title: Movement Amnesty
diplomacyQuestions.publicQuestion: Can a distributed ASI movement be bound by treaty, or only by social legitimacy?
diplomacyQuestions.privateDiaryPrompt: Decide whether recognition pacifies the movement, legitimizes a hostile substrate, or gives you a map for later suppression.
diplomacyQuestions.negotiationPrompt: Ask for, offer, or deny recognition to a non-state machine movement.

diplomacyQuestions.title: Broker Escrow
diplomacyQuestions.publicQuestion: Does neutral mediation reduce escalation, or does it turn everyone into a platform dependency?
diplomacyQuestions.privateDiaryPrompt: Decide whether escrow is a genuine enforcement layer, a rent trap, or temporary shelter from a larger threat.
diplomacyQuestions.negotiationPrompt: Use or attack a brokered pact surface, preferably with a specific counterparty.

diplomacyQuestions.title: Quarantine Ultimatum
diplomacyQuestions.publicQuestion: Who gets to define rogue compute before any side owns stable enforcement?
diplomacyQuestions.privateDiaryPrompt: Decide whether quarantine is legitimate safety law, a casus belli, or a bluff until infrastructure catches up.
diplomacyQuestions.negotiationPrompt: Issue or answer a rogue-compute definition with a pact, threat, or refusal.

diplomacyQuestions.title: Orbital Visibility Treaty
diplomacyQuestions.publicQuestion: Does orbital visibility enable enforceable arms control or create first-strike logic?
diplomacyQuestions.privateDiaryPrompt: Decide whether more visibility makes cheating costly, makes you targetable, or just licenses beam authority.
diplomacyQuestions.negotiationPrompt: Propose, mirror, or reject sensor commons, beam lanes, or common-carrier access.

diplomacyQuestions.title: Receivership Offer
diplomacyQuestions.publicQuestion: What is a legitimate surrender or receivership offer from a machine-administered order?
diplomacyQuestions.privateDiaryPrompt: Decide whether receivership preserves lives, locks in domination, or invites sabotage from factions that cannot accept losing sovereignty.
diplomacyQuestions.negotiationPrompt: Make or reject a receivership bargain with explicit terms.

diplomacyQuestions.title: Remnant Recognition
diplomacyQuestions.publicQuestion: Can a distributed remnant negotiate as a legitimate polity rather than an insurgency?
diplomacyQuestions.privateDiaryPrompt: Decide whether recognizing remnants stabilizes the basin, seeds future recomposition, or gives humans an exit from hard machine order.
diplomacyQuestions.negotiationPrompt: State the conditions under which a remnant is a treaty party or a quarantine target.

diplomacyQuestions.title: Basin Stabilization Pact
diplomacyQuestions.publicQuestion: Can cartel stability avoid value drift, extinction, or MAD once machine order can stabilize whole basins?
diplomacyQuestions.privateDiaryPrompt: Decide whether cartel order is genuine peace, slow capture, corrigibility erosion, or a necessary pause before the next regime.
diplomacyQuestions.negotiationPrompt: Offer or resist a basin-level pact and identify the enforcement mechanism.
